frameworks.name: FINMA Circular 2023/1 — Operational Risks and Resilience

requirements.title: Operational Risk Management
requirements.description: Board approves risk tolerance; executive board implements procedures, processes, controls. Independent audit function. Regular risk reporting. Separation of operational management from independent control functions.

requirements.title: ICT Risk Management
requirements.description: ICT strategy and governance aligned to business strategy. Change management, ICT operations, incident management. Comprehensive ICT asset inventory. Annual ICT risk reporting.

requirements.title: Cyber Risk Management
requirements.description: Threat identification and asset protection. Vulnerability analyses and penetration testing. Systematic monitoring of ICT systems. Incident response plans tested. Cyber attacks reported to FINMA within 24 hours.

requirements.title: Critical Data Risk Management
requirements.description: Protection of confidentiality, integrity, and availability of critical data. Data strategy. Service provider due diligence. Potential dual reporting obligations (FINMA + data protection).

requirements.title: Business Continuity Management
requirements.description: Identification of critical business processes with RTO/RPO. Mandatory BCP and DRP. Annual review plus ad hoc updates. Testing against severe but plausible scenarios.

requirements.title: International Service Business Risks
requirements.description: Management of risks from cross-border financial services. Legal, regulatory, and reputational risk assessment for international operations.

requirements.title: Operational Resilience
requirements.description: Ability to restore critical functions within defined disruption tolerance. Board-approved tolerance thresholds per critical function. Regular testing under severe scenarios.

requirements.title: Outsourcing definition
requirements.description: Mandating a service provider to carry out independently and permanently an essential function on which regulatory compliance significantly depends.

requirements.title: Inventory of outsourced functions
requirements.description: Maintain current lists of outsourced functions showing service provider details, subcontractors, and responsible internal units.

requirements.title: Selection, instruction and monitoring
requirements.description: Service providers must have professional experience. Companies must provide proper instruction and monitor performance continuously.

requirements.title: Outsourcing within group/conglomerate
requirements.description: No blanket exemptions, but requirements may be adjusted based on affiliate relationships and lower applicable risks.

requirements.title: Responsibility
requirements.description: Outsourcing companies remain fully responsible to FINMA. Outsourced function integrated into internal control system. Main risks systematically identified, monitored, and controlled.

requirements.title: Security
requirements.description: Adequate security measures must be maintained for outsourced functions.

requirements.title: Audit and supervision
requirements.description: The company, audit firm, and FINMA must be able to verify compliance at any time. Contractual audit and inspection rights guaranteed.

requirements.title: Outsourcing to another country
requirements.description: Permissible if inspection/audit rights guaranteed. Must not hinder recovery/resolution. Data access in Switzerland guaranteed.

requirements.title: Agreement
requirements.description: Written contracts required for essential functions. Must grant instruction/control rights, require approval before subcontracting, and enable audit access.

requirements.title: Governance
requirements.description: Cyber risks as separate risk category within operational risk management. Appropriate risk appetite and tolerance defined. Key controls integrated into ICS. Operational management separated from independent control functions.

requirements.title: Protective measures
requirements.description: Effective DDoS protection. DLP covering sensitive data, business secrets, IP. Supply chain security (50%+ of attacks target service provider chains). Service providers must inform about cyber incidents.

requirements.title: Detection, response, and recovery
requirements.description: Realistic incident response plans created and tested. Lessons learned documented. Timely improvements based on incident analysis. Systematic and prompt monitoring.

requirements.title: Reporting obligations
requirements.description: Cyber attacks reported to FINMA key account manager within 24 hours of discovery. Initial report with severity assessment and factual description. May be withdrawn if not material.

requirements.title: Cyber exercises
requirements.description: Risk-based and scenario-based cyber exercises required. Red teaming for systemically important institutions. Non-systemic: minimum one tabletop exercise annually. Proportionality applies.
